title: Robots in Our Lives
passage:
Robots in Our Lives

Robots are smart machines. They can do many different things to help humans. Today, we can see robots in houses, schools, hospitals, and factories. They make our lives easier and faster.

In houses, some people have small cleaning robots. These robots are round and flat. They move on the floor and clean dust and dirt. They can work when people are asleep or at work. These robots save a lot of time for busy families. People do not need to clean their floors every day.

In schools, some teachers use small robots to teach young children. These robots can talk, sing, and dance. Children like playing with them. The robots help children learn English, science, and math. It is a very fun way to study. Many schools in Japan and Korea use these teaching robots.

In big factories, there are very large robots. These robots are strong and fast. They can lift heavy boxes and build cars. They do not get tired, and they do not need to sleep. They can work all day and all night. This helps factories make things quickly and cheaply.

In some hospitals, doctors use robots too. These robots carry medicine and food to different rooms. They can also clean the hospital rooms to keep them clean and safe. Some special robots even help doctors during difficult medical operations.

In the future, we will see many more robots. They will do more difficult jobs for us. However, robots cannot do everything. They cannot feel emotions like love, sadness, or happiness. They also cannot think like humans. They still need humans to program them and tell them what to do.

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: What shape are the cleaning robots used in houses?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 states: 'These robots are round and flat.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: Which countries use teaching robots in schools?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 states: 'Many schools in Japan and Korea use these teaching robots.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: What can large factory robots build?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 4 states: 'They can lift heavy boxes and build cars.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 6 states: 'They cannot feel emotions like love, sadness, or happiness.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Cleaning robots can work when people are sleeping.

options.TRUE: If the statement agrees with the information
options.FALSE: If the statement contradicts the information
options.NOT GIVEN: If there is no information on this

questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 states: 'They can work when people are asleep or at work.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Teaching robots are very popular in America.

options.TRUE: If the statement agrees with the information
options.FALSE: If the statement contradicts the information
options.NOT GIVEN: If there is no information on this

questions.explanation: The passage only mentions Japan and Korea using teaching robots. It does not mention America.

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Factory robots need to sleep at night.

options.TRUE: If the statement agrees with the information
options.FALSE: If the statement contradicts the information
options.NOT GIVEN: If there is no information on this

questions.explanation: Paragraph 4 states: 'They do not get tired, and they do not need to sleep.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Robots in hospitals can carry medicine and food.

options.TRUE: If the statement agrees with the information
options.FALSE: If the statement contradicts the information
options.NOT GIVEN: If there is no information on this

questions.explanation: Paragraph 5 states: 'These robots carry medicine and food to different rooms.'

questions.instruction: Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: Teaching robots help children learn English, science, and ______.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 states: 'The robots help children learn English, science, and math.'

questions.instruction: Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: Large robots in factories can lift heavy ______.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 4 states: 'They can lift heavy boxes and build cars.'

questions.instruction: Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: Robots cannot think, so they still need ______ to program them.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 6 states: 'They still need humans to program them and tell them what to do.'

vocabulary.meaning: lập trình, cài đặt hướng dẫn cho máy tính/robot

tactics:
Hướng dẫn làm bài đạt điểm cao (A1-A2)

1. Tìm từ khóa trực tiếp: Ở trình độ A1-A2, từ vựng trong câu hỏi thường giống hệt 100% từ vựng trong bài đọc. Hãy dùng bút chì gạch chân các từ khóa nổi bật (ví dụ: 'round and flat', 'Japan and Korea', 'heavy') rồi quét nhanh (scanning) mắt qua bài đọc để tìm vị trí của chúng.

2. Mẹo làm câu hỏi True/False/Not Given: Nếu thông tin trong câu hỏi hoàn toàn không được nhắc tới trong bài (ví dụ như nước 'America' ở câu 6), đừng suy đoán mà hãy chọn ngay NOT GIVEN.

3. Giới hạn từ điền: Luôn chú ý yêu cầu 'NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS' (Không quá 2 từ). Từ cần điền phải được lấy trực tiếp từ bài đọc, không tự ý thay đổi dạng từ.
